description_long: This is a rectangular stone pedestal with a weathered, classical appearance, likely carved from limestone or marble. The front face features a large, inscribed panel with Latin text, framed by a raised border. One side displays a relief sculpture of a robed female figure with a child, while the opposite side shows a relief of a standing armored male figure, possibly a soldier or deity. The rear face is plain and unadorned, showing significant surface wear and discoloration. The top of the block is recessed, forming a shallow basin or cavity, and the base is broad and slightly uneven, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The overall form and decorative elements indicate it may have served as a commemorative altar or monument in a public or ceremonial space.
description: A weathered rectangular stone pedestal with Latin inscriptions and relief carvings of human figures, featuring a recessed top and a broad, aged base.
description_view_0: The front view shows a large inscribed panel with Latin text framed by a raised border.
description_view_1: This side view displays a relief sculpture of a robed female figure with a child.
description_view_2: The rear view is plain, showing significant surface wear and discoloration with no carvings or inscriptions.
description_view_3: This side view features a relief of a standing armored male figure, possibly a soldier or deity.
attribute_location_context: ancient public square or temple courtyard
attribute_installation_method: set on a prepared stone or earth foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows discoloration, chipped corners, and general erosion consistent with long-term outdoor exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible Latin inscriptions on the front and relief carvings of human figures on the sides.
